LAW AND MOTION 302, THE LAW OFFICE OF PAUL H. NATHAN'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF MUSA JARADIE IS OFF CALENDAR. THERE IS NO PROOF OF SERVICE ON FILE AND MOVANT FAILED TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES IN COMPLIANCE WITH SF LOCAL RULE 2.7B. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 =302/RU
MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD (TRANSACTION ID # 100051606) FILED BY PLAINTIFF JARADIE, MUSA HEARING SET FOR NOV-26-2018 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100040728) FILED BY DEFENDANT ZHANG, YIHANG JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 7.0 DAYS
EXHIBIT 1-11 (TRANSACTION ID # 100047637) FILED BY DEFENDANT ZHANG, YIHANG

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS WITH THE MODIFICATION THAT THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE: MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE COURT FIRST NOTES THAT DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY FILED TWO MOTIONS, FIRST ON JULY 2 AND THEN ON JULY 5, THAT APPEAR TO BE IDENTICAL WITH THE ONLY NOTABLE DIFFERENCE BEING THE TIME OF THE HEARING ON DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY'S MOTION(S), AND THE MOVING PAPERS FILED ON JULY 5 PROVIDED THE WRONG TIME FOR THE HEARING. THE PROOFS OF SERVICE ACCOMPANYING MOVING PAPERS ARE DEFECTIVE, AND DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH C.C.P. SECS. 1005 AND 1013. THE COURT ALSO NOTE THAT DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROPERLY SERVE THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE COURT'S JUNE 10, 2019 ORDER UPON DEFENDANT. IT APPEARS DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY HAS BEEN SERVING PAPERS TO PLAINTIFF BUT ADDRESSING THE PAPERS TO SOMEONE OTHER THAN PLAINTIFF. AS SUCH, COURT HAS SERIOUS CONCERNS ABOUT PLAINTIFF'S DUE PROCESS RIGHTS NOT BEING PROTECTED/PRESERVED UNDER THESE CIRCUMSTANCES. DEFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT YIHANG ZHANG'S MOTION TO COMPEL ENFORCEMENT OF THE COURT'S MARCH 5, 2019, DISCOVERY ORDER AND IMPOSING TERMINATING, ISSUE, EVIDENTIARY, AND MONETARY SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT STIPULATES TO THE MOTION BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THOSE WHO HAVE NOT MADE AN APPEARANCE ARE DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT RULES THE MOTION IS UNOPPOSED AND IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF IS BARRED FROM INTRODUCING ANY EVIDENCE THAT WOULD HAVE BEEN RESPONSIVE TO ANY OF DEFENDANT'S WRITTEN DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND IS FURTHER BARRED FROM OFFERING WITNESS TESTIMONY FROM ANY WITNESS WHOSE IDENTITY WAS SOUGHT IN WRITTEN DISCOVERY. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF. THE MOVING PARTY WILL SUBMIT A PROPOSED FOR OF ORDER THAT INCLUDES MODIFICATIONS DISCUSSED. JUDGE PRO TEM: THOMAS COHEN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT YIHANG ZHANG'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER IMPOSING TERMINATING SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AND DISMISSES PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT WITHOUT PREJUDICE PURSUANT TO CCP 2023.030(D)(3) FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS DATED MARCH 5, 2019 AND JUNE 10, 2019. IN ADDITION, THE SEPTEMBER 9, 2019 TRIAL DATE IS VACATED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE PRO TEM: AARON MINNIS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT YIHANG ZHANG's Motion For An Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. The Court grants the motion and dismisses Plaintiff's complaint without prejudice pursuant to CCP 2023.030(d)(3) for failure to comply with the court's orders dated March 5, 2019 and June 10, 2019.

In addition, the September 9, 2019 trial date is vacated.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Notice And Motion To Compel Enforcement Of The Courts June 10,2019 Discovery Order Imposing Terminating Sanction
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. CATHERINE A. WALSH, ESQ. of Law Office of Catherine A. Walsh (Tel: 925-825-9500) appearing via CourtCall for defendant Yihang Zhang;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT YIHANG ZHANG'S Motion To Compel Enforcement Of The Courts June 10,2019 Discovery Order Imposing Terminating Sanction.

Defense counsel stipulate to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows with the modification that the motion is denied without prejudice: Motion is denied without prejudice. The court first notes that Defendant/moving party filed two motions, first on July 2 and then on July 5, that appear to be identical with the only notable difference being the time of the hearing on Defendant/moving party's motion(s), and the moving papers filed on July 5 provided the wrong time for the hearing. The proofs of service accompanying moving papers are defective, and Defendant/moving party failed to comply with C.C.P. Secs. 1005 and 1013. The court also note that Defendant failed to properly serve the notice of entry of the Court's June 10, 2019 order upon defendant. It appears Defendant/moving party has been serving papers to Plaintiff but addressing the papers to someone other than Plaintiff. As such, court has serious concerns about Plaintiff's due process rights not being protected/preserved under these circumstances. Defense counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Notice And Motion To Compel Enforcement Of The Courts March 5, 2019, Discovery Order And Imposing Terminating, Issue, Evidentiary, And Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Erika K. Bornstein In Supp
Off Calendar Jun-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT YIHANG ZHANG'S MOTION TO COMPEL ENFORCEMENT OF THE COURT'S MARCH 5, 2019, DISCOVERY ORDER AND IMPOSING TERMINATING, ISSUE, EVIDENTIARY, AND MONETARY SANCTIONS

Appearances: Erika K. Bornstein of the Law Office of Catherine A. Walsh (925.825.9500) for defendant and moving party; No other appearances.

Defendant stipulates to the motion being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. Those who have not made an appearance are deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. Discussed and the Court rules the motion is unopposed and is granted as follows: Plaintiff is barred from introducing any evidence that would have been responsive to any of Defendant's written discovery requests and is further barred from offering witness testimony from any witness whose identity was sought in written discovery. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff. The moving party will submit a proposed for of order that includes modifications discussed. Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
